body:
## Plan

## Problem

When Ralph processes an issue, commits and branches are not appearing as linked items on the GitHub issue page. This is because the commit message format doesn't use GitHub's [closing keywords](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/writing-on-github/working-with-advanced-formatting/using-keywords-in-issues-and-pull-requests).

**Root cause:** In `coder.py` line 92, the commit message is:
```python
commit_msg = f"ralph: fix issue #{issue_num}"  # e.g. "ralph: fix issue #3"
```

GitHub recognizes `Fixes #N`, `Closes #N`, `Resolves #N` **only at the start of a line** (or after a bullet). The current format `"ralph: fix issue #3"` has "fix" lowercase and buried after `ralph:` — so GitHub treats it as plain text with no issue association.

The PR body (`ship.py`) already uses `Fixes #{issue_num}`, so the PR *itself* links to the issue. But **individual commits** show under "Linked pull requests" only when they also contain a closing keyword.

## Files to Change

### 1. `ralph/coder.py` (line 92)

**Change the commit message to use `Fixes #N` keyword at the start:**

```python
# Before:
commit_msg = f"ralph: fix issue #{issue_num}"

# After:
commit_msg = f"Fixes #{issue_num}: ralph automated fix"
```

This makes each commit automatically associate with the issue in GitHub's UI (visible under "Linked issues" → "Adds commit" on the issue page, even before PR merge).

### 2. `ralph/ship.py` (lines 29 and 57) — PR title

**Add issue reference to PR title for better visibility:**

```python
# Before (line 28):
"--title", f"Fix: {title}",

# After:
"--title", f"Fixes #{issue_num}: {title}",
```

And the API fallback (line 56):
```python
# Before:
"title": f"Fix: {title}",

# After:
"title": f"Fixes #{issue_num}: {title}",
```

This makes the PR title itself a closing keyword, ensuring the association works even if the body parsing is skipped.

## Risks

- **Low risk.** This is purely a formatting change to commit/PR messages. No logic or workflow changes.
- If a repo has branch protection rules that parse commit message prefixes (e.g., "ralph:" for DCO/conventional commit), removing that prefix could break those rules. However, this is unlikely for a bot workflow.
- The `Fixes` keyword will auto-close the issue when the PR merges. This is the desired behavior. If users want manual merge control, they can use `References #N` instead — but `Fixes` is the standard convention for bot-generated PRs.

_Reply with `/fix` to start coding._
<!-- autoswe-bot -->
